source_text: Scheduling problem formulation.Given a heterogeneous cluster of GPUs, we consider the problem of jointly scheduling multiple models under time-varying workloads.Let D = {d1, . . . , dK} denote a set of Kheteroge- neous GPU devices, where each device d has a memory capacity mdand distinct compute characteristics.Let M = {M1, . . . , MZ} denote a set of Zmodels.Each model Mzmay serve multiple types of workloads (e.g., inference requests with different input sizes or other requirements), denoted as Wz= {wz,1, . . . , wz,Jz}.

translation: We consider a discrete time horizon of Ntimestamps.At each timestamp i âˆˆ{1, . . . , N}, the system observes a workload demand Wiacross all models and workload types.A scheduling plan at timestamp i is denoted as Ïƒi, whic

source_text: We consider a discrete time horizon of Ntimestamps.At each timestamp i âˆˆ{1, . . . , N}, the system observes a workload demand Wiacross all models and workload types.A scheduling plan at timestamp i is denoted as Ïƒi, which specifies (i) the assignment of models and workloads to devices, and (ii) the parallel execution configuration, i.e., data, pipeline, or tensor parallelism, for each model.In our context, the data parallelism means the number of inference replicas.
